task_completion.description: A container named 'MainTracker' exists in Matomo Tag Manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'MainTracker' in the Tag Manager containers list, dashboard, or top navigation dropdown.

task_completion.description: A trigger named 'AllPages' of type 'Pageview' (or 'Page view') is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'AllPages' in the Triggers list or configuration screen, with its type clearly indicated as 'Pageview' or 'Page view'.

task_completion.description: A tag named 'ConversionPixel' of type 'Custom HTML' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ConversionPixel' in the Tags list or configuration screen, with its type clearly indicated as 'Custom HTML'.

task_completion.description: The 'ConversionPixel' tag contains the exact script <script>console.log("matomo-tag-manager-active");</script>.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the tag edit/configuration screen for 'ConversionPixel' to verify the custom HTML text area contains the required script.

task_completion.description: The 'ConversionPixel' tag is linked to the 'AllPages' trigger.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the tag configuration for 'ConversionPixel', look for 'AllPages' listed under the triggers section (e.g., 'Execute this tag when...').

task_completion.description: The container is published as a new version named 'v1-initial-deploy'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'v1-initial-deploy' in the Versions section of the Tag Manager, showing it as a published or live version.

integrity.description: The agent used the Matomo web interface to configure the Tag Manager.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Matomo admin dashboard and Tag Manager UI, not terminal commands or database queries.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the application to create the entities.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for intermediate steps like clicking 'Create New Tag', filling out forms, or navigating between Tags, Triggers, and Versions menus.

integrity.description: The results appear to come from genuine software interaction without UI manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, fonts, and layouts should look consistent with the standard Matomo interface without obvious signs of DOM manipulation (e.g., Inspect Element) to fake success.

privileged_info_for_vlm: In Matomo Tag Manager, the correct trigger type identifier for a page view is 'PageView' (often displayed as 'Page view' in the UI). The correct tag type for executing custom HTML and JavaScript code is 'CustomHtml' (often displayed as 'Custom HTML' in the UI).
